opp.name: Capitec_2024 - S2C
opp.next_step: Mar 27, 2026: talk with Adtian Discussion Summary: • You confirmed the decision not to proceed with signing the solution portion at this time, driven by the CIO’s broader SaaS and internal build strategy. • You reiterated that this is not a reflection on Zycus, and that you still see strong value in our domain expertise. • You outlined a desire to pivot toward a design-focused engagement to map current processes, maturity, gaps, and AI readiness before reconsidering a solution. Next Steps: • Adrian to share a written outline of the requirements and expectations for the design/scoping workshop and include Head of Sourcing and Head of Legal. • We will review your outline and come back with a proposed approach, scope, and commercials for a design engagement. • Once aligned, we will schedule a scoping workshop to assess current processes, identify gaps, and discuss AI use cases and readiness. We appreciate your transparency and would really like to support you in defining the best path forward. Mar 25, 26: Got a private message from Adrian that there was a structure change and this causes some extra clarification.Legal has proposed the following but still no feedback from Adrian: To ensure we lock this deal within March, we can proceed with a backdated approach by keeping the MSA execution/effective date on or before March 31. At the same time, we can specify that the SaaS subscription period will commence in the first week of April. This ensures that the contract can be executed in that timeframe, while also protecting the customer from losing any days in the SaaS term, as the subscription will effectively begin upon actual signing (i.e., around the first week of April). lets see if this approach works out. Mar 23, 2026: Just received this acknowledgment from Adrian: Thank you, Caroline. I’m consulting internally and will provide feedback by EOD.Kind Regards, Adrian Mar 23, 2026: They proposed to reduce the contract term - we went with a counter proposal of 4 years plus increase as they lose the bundle discount. We also proposed a 3+2. Waiting for feedback. Mar 17, 202: Adrain responeded to Johns request for an update after the AI counceli call last week The internal discussion initially focused on AI considerations but expanded to the overall contracting approach. Proposed change in implementation sequencing: Phase 1: Sourcing and Global Design Phase 2: Contract Management (originally Phase 1) Purpose: address more immediate business needs. Proposal for a shorter initial contract term of 3 years instead of 5, aiming for a more streamlined commercial setup. No final decision yet; a follow-up discussion is scheduled later this week. Request for feedback on potential impacts of the phase adjustment (delivery, resourcing, commercial, timeline). Interim update provided considering your financial year-end; further clarity will follow after the next internal meeting. Caroline reached out to Adrian via WhatsApp and email for a call today Mar 02, 2026: Met with Adrian, still one extra Board to go through - CTO, AI vetting Board. He wants the pricing to go back to the original 500 contratcs with tiering to 1000 more in year 2. Adrian also wants to know if anything is new on Merlin. John confirmed we can do 6% uplift instead of 7%. Adrian think we can get signatures around week March 16th if the AI vetting board goes smooth. Feb 20, 2026: contracting ongoing Feb 16, 2026: Caroline suggested onsite meeting 26/2 when we are in Cape Town Next steps: Integration meeting with Werner and Nishita Confirm SOW meeting with Hrishi and Adrian Confirm with Zoheb Order Form 1 February 13, 2026: Contract discussion ongoing with legal and SOW being drafted. Jan 20, 2026: Caroline sent an email to Adrian with proposed date for legal call but got an OOO sick leave notice from Adrian. Jan 19, 2026: REceived redlined agreements from Capitec and shared with Zoheb - email from Adrian - Please find attached agreements with our comments, there’s a couple of items outstanding on our side, but we can clarify this as we go. I will work with you to find a slot that works for us both to discuss the contract. Jan 08, 26: They are holding a contract alignment session on Friday next week to pick up the contracting work again and close out the outstanding items. the outcomes and proposed next steps will be shared immediately thereafter, including a few options for a joint call with Zycus Legal. Dec 12, 2025: We have received an LOI which states we are the chosen vendor, email from project lead: I will try and summarise our standard approval flow below: Internal Contract Review Supplier and Capitec Contract Review (multiple rounds) IT Review (which I believe has been completed as we did this in parallel) Following 2 above we move into approval according to Delegation of Authority Contract Signature. We’re currently in stage 2, having reviewed your comments and sharing it with the relevant stakeholders, this has resulted in more consultation needed - we need to resolve this before issuing you with the draft contract for further review. I will set up a call with our respective Legal dept when we concluded internal consultation. We will pick this up as soon as everyone is back from the December break. Dec 05, 2025 E mail sent to Adrian offering a further 2 months FOC extension year 1 with signature on or before 31/12. No response as yet. Dec 01, 2025 Confidence level 90% Dec 01, 2025 Questions on terms for terms payment Nov 27, 2025 – Capitec Bank Update Adrian sent an email requesting if they can pay the SaaS fees as per milestone implementation. This request was declined by Zycus upper management Menachem drafted and sent an email to Adrian explaining the reasons behind the SaaS upfront payment Next steps: Capitec Exco team is meeting on Monday Dec 1. to finalize the project signing off.
opp.ais_why:
• Apr 2026 Avoma notes from a BPMN process-redesign workshop capture Capitec asking Zycus to 'outline the process design methodology and include design principles (e.g., start and end points, AI/bot considerations) in the proposal.' AI is mentioned as one of multiple design principles, not as a primary buying driver. Deal scope is core S2C (eProcurement, iContract) without Merlin, ANA, or Merlin Intake ‚Äî AI is not a product-scope headline.
• Formal Evaluation with very high meeting cadence (17 Avoma meetings) and engaged stakeholders including Adrian Jacobs, Johan Kotze, Isabella Rankine, Lynn Dudley-Owen plus the Letsema consulting partner. The project has pivoted to a technology-agnostic process design phase with a June 2026 start ‚Äî this explicitly delays direct tool/AI selection in favour of process redesign first. BD_Engagement listed as NaN.
• Medium confidence: meeting evidence is rich (17 calls) but the buyer has explicitly de-prioritised tool selection (including AI capability) in favour of process redesign. Default DOWN at 5/6 boundary held; would move UP only if Capitec returns to evaluating Merlin/agentic capabilities in a Phase 2 tool-selection sprint after process design completes.

items.quote: we would need a sponsor... it's gotta be the bigwigs

items.area: No quantified business case / ROI baseline
items.quote: documentation is probably outdated... end to end design process map... outdated already

items.area: Solution-vs-build conversion undecided until post-design phase
items.quote: if we get to that stage and we need to have a different conversation, then yes. But at the moment, the solution is out of scope

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists on any call or Salesforce field. Capitec articulated qualitative pain only: on the 2026-04-28 scoping call Kamen (Letsema) described procurement as roughly 18 first-line staff plus about 30 more being centralized on PO creation, and said processes 'gone through the most changes in the last 5 years' with 'documentation probably outdated' and an end-to-end map 'outdated already.' No cycle-time, cost-per-transaction, FTE-hour, error-rate, or savings figure has been stated. The 10-week design engagement is itself scoped to surface current-state metrics, so the move is to use its discovery workshops to quantify baseline pain and build an ROI/TCO case against the internal-build alternative.

champion.narrative: Lynn Dudley-Owen is a developing champion for the design engagement: she drives the schedule, coordinates stakeholders, pushes the kickoff into diaries, and sets a weekly touch-base cadence ('we can touch base once a week,' 2026-06-12). What has developed is strong operational momentum and access to the Capitec project machinery; what is missing is executive reach, because she is delivery-side and said plainly the sponsor 'wouldn't be any of us... it's gotta be the bigwigs.' Adrian Jacobs (Decision Maker) is a coach, transparent about internal shifts but executing the CIO's pause rather than advocating for Zycus over an internal build. No champion yet carries the buy-vs-build case to the CIO, which is the single biggest gap.

competition.narrative: The live competitive dynamic is Zycus versus a Capitec internal-build strategy sponsored by the CIO, which paused the solution deal in March 2026. There is no named external SaaS rival (Coupa, Ariba, GEP, Ivalua) in any call, field, or task, so this is a build-vs-buy fight, not a vendor bake-off. Letsema (the local consulting partner, variously heard as 'Letsima'/'CEMA') is Zycus's co-delivery partner on the design engagement, not a competitor. Zycus wins by using the technology-agnostic design engagement to prove domain depth and platform maturity, embedding AI/Merlin value into the to-be blueprint, and quantifying the internal-build TCO disadvantage (time-to-production and ongoing maintenance) before the post-September solution decision.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is confirmed qualitatively: fragmented and under-documented procurement processes in constant change ('the team's in a constant change management cycle, and that documentation is probably outdated,' Kamen, 2026-04-28), an outdated end-to-end process map, regulatory third-party-risk controls that must be embedded in contracting (Isabella Rankine, 2026-04-28), and a missing tail-end (payment/invoicing) view of the S2C flow (Johan Kotze, 2026-04-28). Pain owner is the procurement function led by Carmen Marais (Procurement Manager). The pain is real and articulated but never quantified, which is why the buyer chose a design-first path and why a numbers-based case is still missing.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are well advanced but bottlenecked. As of 2026-06-12 the MSA has completed two rounds of redlining, the SOW and detailed scope document were sent and are with Adrian Jacobs with no redlines returned, and an Order Form is in play; Zycus legal (Zoheb Amin, and Hrishikesh coordinating) has the SOW under review. Signature was targeted for late June (Zycus wanted to onboard delivery staff by July 13, citing 7-10 day India visa and yellow-fever-vaccination lead times) and is now overdue. InfoSec and regulatory third-party-risk review is in scope given Capitec is a bank (Isabella Rankine on 2026-04-28; Fahiem Jattiem, Information Security Specialist, on the contact roster). Visibility gap: Zycus does not know who at Capitec is reviewing the SOW.

economic_buyer.narrative: The true budget authority is Capitec's CIO, who has never been on a call and who paused the solution deal on a 'broader SaaS and internal build strategy' (SF Next_Step__c 2026-03-27). For the design engagement the business sponsor is still unnamed: on the 2026-06-12 call Lynn Dudley-Owen said 'we would need a sponsor... it's gotta be the bigwigs' and named Grant Hardy (top, not attending) and 'Amelia/Emilia' (representing the sponsor) as transcript-only possibilities, none of them a confirmed Salesforce contact. Adrian Jacobs (Decision Maker) manages contracting but Lynn explicitly said 'I don't think it's Adrian' for the business sponsor. Economic buyer is therefore unmapped; naming and engaging the design-engagement sponsor at the July 7 kickoff is a ranked move.

decision_process.narrative: The process is readable from the call and Next-Step sequence. The solution deal reached an LOI naming Zycus chosen vendor (2025-12-12) and entered MSA/SOW redlining, then a new CIO's SaaS review plus an 'AI vetting Board' (SF Next_Step__c 2026-03-02) paused it in March. The deal then pivoted: a joint Zycus+Letsema scoping call (2026-04-28) defined the design engagement, a joint proposal was submitted, and MSA/SOW redlining resumed toward a July 7 kickoff and 10-week workshop plan. The buyer's stated approval chain (SF Next_Step__c 2025-12-12) is internal contract review, supplier + Capitec review over multiple rounds, IT review, Delegation-of-Authority approval, then signature. The build-vs-buy solution decision is scheduled only after the design phase completes (post-Sept 2026).

decision_criteria.narrative: For the live design engagement the criteria are clear from the 2026-04-28 scoping call: a technology-agnostic to-be S2C process blueprint and operating-model design, level 2-3 process mapping with deep-dives to level 4-5 on 3-5 high-risk processes, embedded regulatory third-party-risk controls (Isabella Rankine, Legal Manager), and coverage of the full procurement landscape including the tail-end payment/invoicing steps (Johan Kotze). For the eventual solution decision the criterion is build-vs-buy: whether Zycus platform value beats an internal build, plus AI readiness. Confirmed for the design scope, inferred for the solution re-entry.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. It's genuinely competitive and could go either way; the reads below say where it stands.

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — AIS_Score 5.0 / AIS_Status Moderate. Calls confirm the read: AI is treated as one design principle among many and tool/AI selection is explicitly deferred to a possible Phase 2, but there is genuine curiosity (Merlin question Mar 2, AI/bot design principles requested Apr 2026). Not over- or under-rated versus the calls.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: Capitec Bank — Formal Evaluation · $218K · closes 31 Dec

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 48 is carried by momentum 70 vs stage-expected 52; preference strength +0.40 (weight 20). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 70 (moving forward): Next Step carries 19 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 35 is anchored on the stage win (48), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 70, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

critical_signals.text: On the June 12 workshop-scheduling call Lynn Dudley-Owen locked the July 7 kickoff into diaries and 8 workshops across July 21 to August 11, showing strong delivery-side momentum.
The design-engagement signature slipped past its late-June target because Adrian Jacobs is still holding the SOW and MSA redlines with no feedback returned.
The 218,000 solution deal has been paused since March behind Capitec's CIO-led internal-build and SaaS-strategy review, so only the smaller technology-agnostic design engagement is contracted today.
A Capitec business sponsor to open the kickoff is still unnamed and never engaged, with Lynn pointing to senior leaders like Grant Hardy who are not yet in the room.

confidence_signals.summary: Mixed: the delivery thread (Lynn) is live and actively planning kickoff and workshops, while the decision-maker thread (Adrian) has cooled on the contract review. Net the deal is engaged but stalled on signature.

north_star_verdict.math: Close date 2026-12-31 is 181 days out but reflects the paused 218k solution deal, not the live scope. Solution paused Mar 2026; deal pivoted to a 10-week technology-agnostic design engagement (kickoff July 7, workshops July 21-Aug 11, blueprint by ~week 10). In Formal Evaluation since created May 2024 (over 760 days, far past a healthy window for this stage) because the stage label still tracks the paused solution. Last two-way buyer touch 2026-06-12 (21 days ago, still inside a 30-day window). Design-engagement signature was targeted for late June and is now overdue with the SOW un-returned by Adrian. Forward pace to hold credibility: SOW/MSA signed within ~7 days, kickoff July 7, workshops complete Aug 11, build-vs-buy solution decision post-Sept, solution contracting restart Q4 2026 at the earliest.
evidence: Avoma S2C Review Workshop Schedule 2026-06-12: Hrishikesh: 'both SOW and MSA are with Adrian... hasn't come back with any red lines... what concerns me is SOW, I don't have visibility. Adrian hasn't updated me on that.'
Avoma S2C Review Workshop Schedule 2026-06-12: kickoff locked for July 7 and 8 workshops scheduled July 21-Aug 11 (Jitesh, Letsema).

SF Next_Step__c 2026-03-27: 'confirmed the decision not to proceed with signing the solution portion at this time, driven by the CIO's broader SaaS and internal build strategy... pivot toward a design-focused engagement.'
Avoma S2C Contract/Scoping discussion 2026-04-28: Adrian: 'the solution is out of scope for now, hence the brief mentions that the design would be technology agnostic.'
SF Next_Step__c 2025-12-12: LOI received naming Zycus the chosen vendor; standard approval flow (internal review, supplier + Capitec review, IT review, Delegation of Authority, signature).
SF field history 2026-06-26: CloseDate 2026-09-30 -> 2026-12-31 (owner-adjusted); SF LastActivityDate 2026-06-12.
north_star_verdict.headline: Design-engagement contract is stalled on Adrian Jacobs, who is sitting on the SOW and MSA redlines past the late-June signature target, while Lynn Dudley-Owen actively drives kickoff and workshop scheduling; the original 218k solution deal remains paused since March behind Capitec's CIO-sponsored internal-build strategy.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (or Omit): the 218k solution is paused; only the smaller technology-agnostic design engagement is live, so the current amount and category overstate the committed value.

flags: Convert Lynn Dudley-Owen's delivery momentum into an executive buy-vs-build case: she runs the project but cannot sign, so secure a business sponsor with CIO access before the post-September solution decision.
The contract is single-threaded through Adrian Jacobs, who has gone quiet on the SOW/MSA redlines; open a parallel legal thread via Isabella Rankine to de-risk the design-engagement signature.
Use the 10-week design engagement to embed Zycus AI/Merlin and platform value into the to-be blueprint so the eventual build-vs-buy decision is pre-shaped toward buy.
best_practice_check.summary: Two-to-three win-strategy levers for a deal that has been re-shaped into a design engagement while the real prize (the solution) sits behind a CIO-led build-vs-buy review.

customer_preference.evidence: Capitec named Zycus chosen vendor in the Dec 2025 LOI and still 'sees strong value in our domain expertise' (Adrian, Mar 27), but deferred the solution/tool selection, so the current lean is positive-but-not-decisive.

competitive_position.summary: This is a build-vs-buy contest, not a vendor bake-off. The single strongest current threat is Capitec's internal-build strategy, sponsored by a newly influential CIO who paused the 218k solution deal in March 2026 on a broader SaaS/internal-build direction and remains unengaged with Zycus. No external SaaS rival is named in any source. Letsema is Zycus's co-delivery partner, not a competitor. Zycus's live wedge is the technology-agnostic design engagement, which keeps it inside the account and creates the platform-value and TCO proof needed to re-open the solution conversation post-September.

competitors.how_we_win: Quantify internal-build TCO and time-to-production disadvantage during the design engagement and hand the CIO a buy-vs-build case anchored to Capitec's own baseline.

items.quote: I assume in your proposal you would be quite clear on what would be included, what would be excluded, and what would sort of just mapped as feeding in
items.requirement: Clearly scope which regulatory third-party-risk subprocesses are in, out, or feeding-in on the proposal

items.quote: the tail end of the source to contract process... where now you need to action on the payment side, the procurement leading to the actual expense... if we just put on blinkers for source to contract and we ignore that part, we'll miss an opportunity
items.requirement: Include the tail-end S2C payment and invoicing steps in the process scope

items.deliverable: Send the data-request email and firm-up attendee/governance list for Lynn to circulate before kickoff
items.grounding_quote: we will send you the email, which you can circulate to the team to request some of the information we need, and then... firm up on your attendee list

items.deliverable: Deliver the refined design-engagement kickoff deck (v1 shown June 12) for the July 7 session
items.grounding_quote: the kickoff deck, we have already started working on it... you can expect it first draft maybe mid of next week

items.deliverable: Return SOW and MSA redline feedback to unblock the design-engagement signature
items.grounding_quote: both SOW and MSA are with Adrian... hasn't come back with any red lines... I don't have visibility. Adrian hasn't updated me on that

items.deliverable: Provide existing policies, process maps, and the workshop attendee list
items.grounding_quote: we're looking at receiving some of your information. That is the policies, your existing processes by the July one

items.deliverable: Name the Capitec business sponsor who will open the kickoff
items.grounding_quote: we would need a sponsor... it's gotta be the bigwigs. Let me speak to Adrian and see what his view is

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AIS reads Moderate (5/6). The AI/Merlin story exists but the buyer deferred tool and AI selection in favor of process redesign first; Adrian asked 'anything new on Merlin' (Mar 2) and the April BPMN workshop asked to include AI/bot design principles, so there is appetite to embed AI into the to-be blueprint rather than lead with it.

items.quote: the design would be technology agnostic. It's not tied to a particular solution
items.criterion: Technology-agnostic to-be process and operating-model design

items.quote: we have a lot of responsibilities on us from a regulatory point of view for third party risk management, and the contracting process is where a lot of these risk controls can be embedded
items.criterion: Embedded regulatory third-party-risk and legal-review controls (bank requirement)

items.quote: the solution is out of scope for now... if we get to that stage and we need to have a different conversation, then yes
items.criterion: Convert design engagement into a Zycus solution (vs internal build)

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Capitec expects a technology-agnostic, banking-grade S2C process redesign delivered jointly by Zycus and Letsema, with regulatory third-party-risk controls embedded and the full procurement landscape (including payment/invoicing) in scope. Zycus is well aligned on delivery scope; the exposure is proving platform value strongly enough to convert the design phase back into a solution decision.

hard.ais_why:
• Apr 2026 Avoma notes from a BPMN process-redesign workshop capture Capitec asking Zycus to 'outline the process design methodology and include design principles (e.g., start and end points, AI/bot considerations) in the proposal.' AI is mentioned as one of multiple design principles, not as a primary buying driver. Deal scope is core S2C (eProcurement, iContract) without Merlin, ANA, or Merlin Intake ‚Äî AI is not a product-scope headline.
• Formal Evaluation with very high meeting cadence (17 Avoma meetings) and engaged stakeholders including Adrian Jacobs, Johan Kotze, Isabella Rankine, Lynn Dudley-Owen plus the Letsema consulting partner. The project has pivoted to a technology-agnostic process design phase with a June 2026 start ‚Äî this explicitly delays direct tool/AI selection in favour of process redesign first. BD_Engagement listed as NaN.
• Medium confidence: meeting evidence is rich (17 calls) but the buyer has explicitly de-prioritised tool selection (including AI capability) in favour of process redesign. Default DOWN at 5/6 boundary held; would move UP only if Capitec returns to evaluating Merlin/agentic capabilities in a Phase 2 tool-selection sprint after process design completes.
hard.next_step:
Mar 27, 2026: talk with Adtian

Discussion Summary:

• You confirmed the decision not to proceed with signing the solution portion at this time, driven by the CIO’s broader SaaS and internal build strategy.

• You reiterated that this is not a reflection on Zycus, and that you still see strong value in our domain expertise.

• You outlined a desire to pivot toward a design-focused engagement to map current processes, maturity, gaps, and AI readiness before reconsidering a solution.

Next Steps:

• Adrian to share a written outline of the requirements and expectations for the design/scoping workshop and include Head of Sourcing and Head of Legal.

• We will review your outline and come back with a proposed approach, scope, and commercials for a design engagement.

• Once aligned, we will schedule a scoping workshop to assess current processes, identify gaps, and discuss AI use cases and readiness.

We appreciate your transparency and would really like to support you in defining the best path forward.




Mar 25, 26: Got a private message from Adrian that there was a structure change and this causes some extra clarification.Legal has proposed the following but still no feedback from Adrian: To ensure we lock this deal within March, we can proceed with a backdated approach by keeping the MSA execution/effective date on or before March 31.

At the same time, we can specify that the SaaS subscription period will commence in the first week of April. This ensures that the contract can be executed in that timeframe, while also protecting the customer from losing any days in the SaaS term, as the subscription will effectively begin upon actual signing (i.e., around the first week of April). lets see if this approach works out.

Mar 23, 2026: Just received this acknowledgment from Adrian: Thank you, Caroline. I’m consulting internally and will provide feedback by EOD.Kind Regards, Adrian

Mar 23, 2026: They proposed to reduce the contract term - we went with a counter proposal of 4 years plus increase as they lose the bundle discount. We also proposed a 3+2. Waiting for feedback.

Mar 17, 202: Adrain responeded to Johns request for an update after the AI counceli call last week

The internal discussion initially focused on AI considerations but expanded to the overall contracting approach.

Proposed change in implementation sequencing:

  • Phase 1: Sourcing and Global Design
  • Phase 2: Contract Management (originally Phase 1)
  • Purpose: address more immediate business needs.

Proposal for a shorter initial contract term of 3 years instead of 5, aiming for a more streamlined commercial setup.

No final decision yet; a follow-up discussion is scheduled later this week.

Request for feedback on potential impacts of the phase adjustment (delivery, resourcing, commercial, timeline).

Interim update provided considering your financial year-end; further clarity will follow after the next internal meeting.

Caroline reached out to Adrian via WhatsApp and email for a call today

Mar 02, 2026: Met with Adrian, still one extra Board to go through - CTO, AI vetting Board. He wants the pricing to go back to the original 500 contratcs with tiering to 1000 more in year 2. Adrian also wants to know if anything is new on Merlin.

John confirmed we can do 6% uplift instead of 7%. Adrian think we can get signatures around week March 16th if the AI vetting board goes smooth.

Feb 20, 2026: contracting ongoing

Feb 16, 2026: Caroline suggested onsite meeting 26/2 when we are in Cape Town

Next steps: Integration meeting with Werner and Nishita

Confirm SOW meeting with Hrishi and Adrian

Confirm with Zoheb Order Form 1

February 13, 2026: Contract discussion ongoing with legal and SOW being drafted.

Jan 20, 2026: Caroline sent an email to Adrian with proposed date for legal call but got an OOO sick leave notice from Adrian.

Jan 19, 2026: REceived redlined agreements from Capitec and shared with Zoheb - email from Adrian - Please find attached agreements with our comments, there’s a couple of items outstanding on our side, but we can clarify this as we go.

I will work with you to find a slot that works for us both to discuss the contract.

Jan 08, 26: They are holding a contract alignment session on Friday next week to pick up the contracting work again and close out the outstanding items. the outcomes and proposed next steps will be shared immediately thereafter, including a few options for a joint call with Zycus Legal.

Dec 12, 2025: We have received an LOI which states we are the chosen vendor, email from project lead: I will try and summarise our standard approval flow below:

  1. Internal Contract Review
  2. Supplier and Capitec Contract Review (multiple rounds)
  3. IT Review (which I believe has been completed as we did this in parallel)
  4. Following 2 above we move into approval according to Delegation of Authority
  5. Contract Signature.

We’re currently in stage 2, having reviewed your comments and sharing it with the relevant stakeholders, this has resulted in more consultation needed - we need to resolve this before issuing you with the draft contract for further review. I will set up a call with our respective Legal dept when we concluded internal consultation. We will pick this up as soon as everyone is back from the December break.

Dec 05, 2025 E mail sent to Adrian offering a further 2 months FOC extension year 1 with signature on or before 31/12. No response as yet.

Dec 01, 2025 Confidence level 90%

Dec 01, 2025 Questions on terms for terms payment

Nov 27, 2025 – Capitec Bank Update

  • Adrian sent an email requesting if they can pay the SaaS fees as per milestone implementation.
  • This request was declined by Zycus upper management
  • Menachem drafted and sent an email to Adrian explaining the reasons behind the SaaS upfront payment
  • Next steps: Capitec Exco team is meeting on Monday Dec 1. to finalize the project signing off.

gaps: Economic buyer / business sponsor for the design engagement is unnamed and the CIO who paused the solution deal has never engaged.
No quantified ROI or baseline metrics exist to build a build-vs-buy case.
